sections.paragraph:
ἀνασπάω fut. ἀνασπάσω; 1 aor. ἀνέσπασα; 1 aor. pass. ἀνεσπάσθην (s. σπάομαι; Hom. et al.; PTebt 420, 25; BGU 1041, 8; PGM 4, 2498; 2973; Hab 1:15; Da 6:18; Bel 42 Theod.; TestSol 6:13 D; TestAbr A 11 p. 90, 4 [Stone p. 28]; ParJer 3:13; Jos., Ant. 2, 259 al.) draw/pull up Lk 14:5. Of the sheet, which Peter saw in his vision ἀνεσπάσθη εἰς τ. οὐρανόν Ac 11:10 (Pherecrates Com., Fgm. 180 K. κάδους ἀνασπῶν).—M-M.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Com. Com. = Comicorum Atticorum fragmenta (TKock) and related collections of comedy fragments as noted in text; various dates—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 71
